title: biophytum castum
intro: Biophytum castum, also known as manjishtha or Indian gooseberry, is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the family Oxalidaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Biophytum castum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support skin health, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are typically dried and used to make teas, capsules, or tonics, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
